# Scenario 7: Dependency Configurations (What to Do When a Dependency Exists — But Not Where You Expect It)

## What This Scenario Demonstrates
This scenario demonstrates Gradle configurations (`implementation`, `compileOnly`, `testImplementation`).

## Dependency Graph
Standard scopes.

### Gradle (Groovy DSL)

```{.groovy include="/demos/gradle-demo/scenario-7-scopes/build.gradle" snippet="s7-deps"}
```

## Expected Intuition
`compileOnly` is not in runtime.

## Actual Resolution Results
*   **Gradle**: Respects configurations.

## Classpath Reality
`lib-b` (compileOnly) is not in runtime classpath.

## Why This Happens
Configurations define visibility.

## How Developers Commonly "Fix" This
N/A

## Safer Ways to Take Control
Use `api` vs `implementation` correctly in libraries.

## Signals to Watch For
N/A

## How This Scales in Real Systems
Critical for build performance (ABI compatibility).

## Key Takeaway
Configurations control transitivity and packaging.

## Related Scenarios
*   [Scenario 8: Optional](../scenario-8-optional/guide.md)
